slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log highlights gaps in evidence readiness for control closure, requiring archival or verification of pending items to meet compliance standards, while the Privileged Account Approval Email reflects a formally approved, governance-controlled access arrangement with strict operational conditions and ongoing oversight requirements.

- Access Review Evidence Log indicates pending evidence items that may delay control closure; Privileged Account Approval Email confirms active, approved access with defined controls.  
- Evidence in the log must meet ownership, date, and linkage criteria; the email specifies scoping, logging, and quarterly review conditions for access.  
- The log is synthetic and intended for testing; the email reflects real governance with revalidation upon personnel changes.  
- Control closure in the log depends on resolving evidence gaps; the email’s access is conditionally maintained under strict governance.  
- The log emphasizes compliance through evidence completeness; the email emphasizes accountability through conditional approval and recertification.  
- One addresses evidence readiness for audit closure; the other addresses governance of persistent privileged access.
